title: Collision-Based Export Formula Report
subtitle: Formulas implemented in stress_collision_based.py
output_files: Per-collision values at the selected extract time.
notation: Current sample time returned by EDEM for timestep k.
Interval length from the previous sample to the current sample.
Set of collisions present in the current surfSurf sample, not only newly started collisions.
Number of sampled collisions at timestep k.
Start time and end time of collision i reported by EDEM.
Overlap duration of collision i with the interval [t_(k-1), t_k], not the full contact lifetime.
Full contact duration of collision i.
Masses of the two particles in collision i.
Radii of the two particles in collision i.
Current total normal and total tangential force vectors from EDEM.
Magnitudes of normal, tangential, and total collision force used in the code.
Normal, shear, and total collision energies used in the code.
Host particles selected by the configured host material.

equations.label: C1
equations.title: Interval length
equations.description: For the first processed timestep, the implementation uses 0 as the previous time if no earlier sample is available.

equations.label: C2
equations.title: Overlap duration inside the current interval
equations.description: This is the contact time attributed to the current interval for collision i. It is not the same as the full contact duration d_i, which is defined separately.

equations.label: C3
equations.title: Full contact duration of one collision
equations.description: Used for the summary field Ave_contact_duration_per_collision.

equations.label: C4
equations.title: Collision frequency based on new collisions started in the interval
equations.latex: N_k^{new} = \begin{cases} \sum_{i \in C_k} I[t_{k-1} \le s_i \le t_k], & t_{k-1} \le 0, \\ \sum_{i \in C_k} I[t_{k-1} < s_i \le t_k], & t_{k-1} > 0, \end{cases}\quad f_k^{coll} = \frac{N_k^{new}}{\Delta t_k}
equations.description: C_k is the full collision set present in the current sample, not only newly started collisions. N_k^new is the subset count within C_k whose start times fall in the current interval. It follows the count_started_collisions code path: the first interval includes the lower bound so collisions starting exactly at time 0 are counted once, while later intervals use an open lower bound to avoid double counting on timestep boundaries.

equations.label: C5
equations.title: Current force magnitudes
equations.description: The total collision force magnitude is the norm of the vector sum, not the sum of norms.

equations.label: C6
equations.title: Maximum-force magnitude used for impact statistics
equations.description: Used for Ave_Fcol_Max_per_collision, Max_Fcol_Max_per_collision, and high_impact_event_fraction.

equations.label: C7
equations.title: Reduced mass
equations.description: If the denominator is zero, the code treats the reduced mass as invalid and writes zero for specific-intensity terms.

equations.label: C8
equations.title: Collision specific-intensity values
equations.description: Here E_i = E_{n,i} + E_{s,i}. The current code sets dissipation energy to zero.

equations.label: C9
equations.title: Collision area proxy used in stress calculations
equations.description: This is a geometric proxy area from the current implementation, not a Hertz contact patch.

equations.label: C10
equations.title: Trapezoidal impulse for matched sampled collisions
equations.description: The code matches current and previous collisions by unordered particle pair and, when needed, nearest contact position. Only currently sampled collisions that can be matched contribute here; collision-level exports do not add a separate previous-only tail term.

equations.label: C11
equations.title: Collision stress values from impulse, overlap time, and area
equations.description: If overlap time or area is non-positive, the code writes zero. These exported sigma values are stress-like proxies from the implementation, not direct physical contact stresses.

equations.label: C12
equations.title: Collision power values
equations.latex: P_{n,i} = \frac{E_{n,i}}{\Delta t_k},\quad P_{s,i} = \frac{E_{s,i}}{\Delta t_k},\quad P_i = \frac{E_i}{\Delta t_k}
equations.description: Power uses the current interval length in the denominator.

equations.label: C13
equations.title: Aggregate specific energy by collision
equations.latex: SE_{n,k}^{coll} = \frac{\sum_{i \in C_k} E_{n,i}}{\sum_{i \in C_k} m_{eff,i}},\quad SE_{s,k}^{coll} = \frac{\sum_{i \in C_k} E_{s,i}}{\sum_{i \in C_k} m_{eff,i}},\quad SE_k^{coll} = \frac{\sum_{i \in C_k} E_i}{\sum_{i \in C_k} m_{eff,i}}
equations.description: The implementation aggregates energy first and then divides by the sum of valid reduced masses. This is not the arithmetic mean of the per-collision SI values.

equations.label: C14
equations.title: Particle split used for particle SI values still exported in the collision summary CSV
equations.latex: E_{n,p} = \frac{1}{2}\sum_{i \sim p} E_{n,i},\quad E_{s,p} = \frac{1}{2}\sum_{i \sim p} E_{s,i},\quad SI_{n,p} = \frac{E_{n,p}}{m_p},\quad SI_{s,p} = \frac{E_{s,p}}{m_p}
equations.description: These particle-based SI values appear in collision_over_time.csv because the shared timestep core computes both collision and particle summaries.

equations.label: C15
equations.title: Host-particle rotational metrics
equations.latex: d_{h,k}^{rot} = r_h \|\boldsymbol{\omega}_h\| \Delta t_k,\quad \xi_{h,k} = I[n_{h,k} > 0],\quad D_{h,k}^{rot} = D_{h,k-1}^{rot} + \xi_{h,k} d_{h,k}^{rot},\quad T_{h,k}^{contact} = T_{h,k-1}^{contact} + \xi_{h,k}\Delta t_k
equations.description: The contact indicator uses the current sampled active collision count for each host particle. It is a current-step gate, not an overlap-history accumulator by itself.

summary_fields: time
Current sample time t_k.
EDEM time value for the processed timestep.
number_of_collisions
N_k = |C_k|.
Number of sampled collisions in the current surfSurf snapshot.
Ave_contact_duration_per_collision
Mean of d_i over current sampled collisions. Eq. C3.
Average full contact duration.
total_collision_time
Sum of tau_i^overlap over sampled collisions. Eq. C2.
Total overlap time attributed to the current interval.
average_collision_time
Mean of tau_i^overlap over sampled collisions. Eq. C2.
Average overlap time per sampled collision in the current interval.
collision_frequency
N_k^new / Delta t_k. Eq. C4.
Frequency of the newly started subset of C_k, where C_k itself is the full current sampled collision set.
Ave_TotalColForce_inSys_byCollision
Mean of F_i over sampled collisions. Eq. C5.
Average current total collision force magnitude.
Ave_Fcol_Max_per_collision
Mean of F_i^max. Eq. C6.
Average maximum force magnitude.
Max_Fcol_Max_per_collision
Max of F_i^max. Eq. C6.
Largest maximum force magnitude in the sampled collision set.
high_impact_event_fraction
Count(F_i^max > threshold) / N_k. Eq. C6.
Fraction of sampled collisions above the configured impact threshold.
Ave_SI_normal_per_collision
Mean of SI_n,i over valid reduced masses. Eq. C8.
Average collision normal specific intensity.
Ave_SI_shear_per_collision
Mean of SI_s,i over valid reduced masses. Eq. C8.
Average collision shear specific intensity.
Ave_SI_total_per_collision
Mean of SI_i over valid reduced masses. Eq. C8.
Average collision total specific intensity.
Total_normal_specific_energy_by_collision
Aggregate collision normal specific energy. Eq. C13.
Energy summed over collisions, then divided by summed reduced mass.
Total_shear_specific_energy_by_collision
Aggregate collision shear specific energy. Eq. C13.
Energy summed over collisions, then divided by summed reduced mass.
Total_specific_energy_by_collision
Aggregate collision total specific energy. Eq. C13.
Energy summed over collisions, then divided by summed reduced mass.
Ave_SI_normal_per_particle
Mean over particles of SI_n,p from the shared core. Eq. C14.
Particle-based normal SI still written into the collision summary CSV.
Ave_SI_shear_per_particle
Mean over particles of SI_s,p from the shared core. Eq. C14.
Particle-based shear SI still written into the collision summary CSV.
Ave_SI_total_per_particle
Mean over particles of SI_p from the shared core. Eq. C14.
Particle-based total SI still written into the collision summary CSV.
Ave_normal_stress_value_per_collision
Mean of sigma_n,i over sampled collisions. Eq. C11.
Average normal stress proxy per collision.
Ave_shear_stress_value_per_collision
Mean of sigma_s,i over sampled collisions. Eq. C11.
Average shear stress proxy per collision.
Ave_stress_value_per_collision
Mean of sigma_i over sampled collisions. Eq. C11.
Average total stress proxy per collision.
Ave_normal_power_per_collision
Mean of P_n,i over sampled collisions. Eq. C12.
Average normal power per collision.
Ave_shear_power_per_collision
Mean of P_s,i over sampled collisions. Eq. C12.
Average shear power per collision.
Ave_power_per_collision
Mean of P_i over sampled collisions. Eq. C12.
Average total power per collision.
Total_normal_power_by_collision
Sum of P_n,i over sampled collisions. Eq. C12.
Total normal power across the current sampled collision set.
Total_shear_power_by_collision
Sum of P_s,i over sampled collisions. Eq. C12.
Total shear power across the current sampled collision set.
Total_power_by_collision
Sum of P_i over sampled collisions. Eq. C12.
Total power across the current sampled collision set.
Ave_rotational_distance_per_host_particle
Mean of d_h,k^rot over all host particles sampled at k. Eq. C15.
Average host rotational distance in the current interval.
Ave_rotational_distance_total_per_host_particle
Mean of D_h,k^rot over host particles with xi_h,k = 1. Eq. C15.
Average cumulative host rotational distance among currently contacting host particles.
Ave_cumulative_contact_time_per_host_particle
Mean of T_h,k^contact over host particles with xi_h,k = 1. Eq. C15.
Average cumulative host contact time among currently contacting host particles.
extract_fields: Scalar t_k for the extract timestep.
Written once at the top of the extracted block by the CSV expansion helper.
Local collision row number in the current extract snapshot, not a persistent collision ID.
Raw first collision particle ID from EDEM.
First particle in the sampled collision pair.
Raw second collision particle ID from EDEM.
Second particle in the sampled collision pair.
SI_n,i when reduced mass is valid, else 0. Eq. C8.
SI_s,i when reduced mass is valid, else 0. Eq. C8.
SI_i when reduced mass is valid, else 0. Eq. C8.
Collision area proxy used by the stress calculation.
notes: All displayed formulas reflect the current implementation in stress_collision_based.py.
Collision continuity for the trapezoidal rule is matched by unordered particle pair and nearest contact position when multiple contacts share the same pair.
The collision summary and collision extracted file do not add a separate previous-only tail term. Tail handling is only added at particle level inside the shared timestep core.
If a denominator is zero, if no valid term exists, or if a feature flag is disabled in Stress_settings.txt, the corresponding exported values remain 0.
The CSV expansion helper writes scalar fields such as time only once at the top of an extracted block, leaving the remaining rows blank in that scalar column.
